viewsInfo.description: Hierarchical overview that groups retweeting threads by content similarity so analysts can navigate topics and select aggregates or leaf threads.

quotes: the cluster view groups all retweeting threads hierarchically
reveals the content similarities among retweeting threads in a dendrogram
evidence.reasoning: The caption and interface description identify this crop as the cluster overview for hierarchical topic navigation.

subViews.description: Main dendrogram region with circle-encoded thread nodes and compact branch summaries for navigating hierarchical clusters.

quotes: each internal tree node represents an aggregate of related retweeting threads
evidence.reasoning: The cluster crop shows one primary hierarchy region rather than separate named subpanels.

color.description: Interior color of a thread node indicates thread sentiment.
Outline color of a thread node indicates anomaly score.

source.description: Dendrogram links connect parent aggregates to child nodes.

target.description: Dendrogram links connect parent aggregates to child nodes.

quotes: Initially all nodes in the cluster view are represented with small circles
evidence.reasoning: Text and crop support circle-encoded nodes within a dendrogram structure.

capabilities.description: Shows content similarity structure among retweeting threads so analysts can navigate topics and aggregates.

quotes: allowing users to navigate the data in a sense of topics and keywords
evidence.reasoning: The cluster view directly provides hierarchical topic context.

capabilities.description: Lets the user collapse nodes below a chosen hierarchy level using a toolbar slider.

quotes: allows a user to quickly collapse all nodes below certain tree level
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly describes level-based collapsing in the cluster view.

capabilities.description: Supports on-demand inspection of metadata and tweet content for a thread node through tooltips and context menus.

quotes: Both views provide informative tooltips (Fig. 5-g) and context menus (Fig. 5-h)
evidence.reasoning: The cluster overview is one of the two explicit sources for the auxiliary detail surfaces.

capabilities.description: Double-clicking a node unfolds the selected thread in the threads view.

quotes: double-click a node in either to unfold the timeline visualization of that thread in the threads view
evidence.reasoning: The cluster overview explicitly triggers thread loading into the timeline workspace.

capabilities.description: Hovering descendant nodes highlights corresponding retweets within a loaded thread timeline.

quotes: highlight retweets from each of its sub-threads by hovering over the descendant nodes
evidence.reasoning: The passage explicitly names the cluster view as a source of cross-view highlight interaction.

viewsInfo.description: Projection overview showing thread distributions in anomaly feature space for outlier finding and comparison.

quotes: the MDS view summarizes the relationships of threads in a high-dimensional feature space
allowing users to identify outliers and visually compare the threads
evidence.reasoning: The crop and text describe one projection-based overview for the anomaly feature space.

subViews.description: Scatterplot-like projection of thread nodes in a two-dimensional MDS embedding of the anomaly feature space.

quotes: shows the distributions of threads with MDS projection from the high-dimensional anomaly feature space
evidence.reasoning: The MDS view is a single named projection region.

x.description: Horizontal position comes from MDS projection of the anomaly feature space.

y.description: Vertical position comes from MDS projection of the anomaly feature space.

color.description: Thread nodes reuse the same color encodings as the cluster view.

quotes: The MDS view applies consistent visual encodings to the thread nodes as the cluster view
evidence.reasoning: The view is a point-based MDS projection reusing node encodings from the cluster overview.

capabilities.description: Shows relative positions of threads in anomaly feature space so analysts can identify outliers and compare groups.

quotes: allowing users to identify outliers and visually compare the threads
evidence.reasoning: The MDS view directly provides high-level comparison and outlier context.

capabilities.description: Supports basic navigation of the MDS layout through zooming and panning.

quotes: supports several basic interactions such as zooming and panning
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly lists zoom and pan in the MDS view.

capabilities.description: Supports on-demand access to tooltip and context-menu details for a thread node.

quotes: Both views provide informative tooltips (Fig. 5-g) and context menus (Fig. 5-h)
evidence.reasoning: The MDS overview is explicitly cited as a source of details-on-demand.

capabilities.description: Double-clicking a projected node unfolds the selected thread in the threads view.

quotes: double-click a node in either to unfold the timeline visualization of that thread in the threads view
evidence.reasoning: The overview-detail transition is explicitly supported from the MDS view.

capabilities.description: Hovering descendant nodes highlights the corresponding retweets inside loaded thread timelines.

quotes: hovering over the descendant nodes in either the cluster view or the MDS view
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly names the MDS view as a source of cross-view highlighting.

processingSteps.name: multidimensional scaling (MDS)
processingSteps.description: Projects retweeting threads from the high-dimensional anomaly feature space into a 2D layout for overview comparison and outlier inspection.

quotes: shows the distributions of threads with MDS projection from the high-dimensional anomaly feature space
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly binds an MDS projection to the MDS overview.

viewsInfo.description: Central timeline view for comparing retweeting threads, their user activity, hidden states, and social overlays across multiple temporal scales.

quotes: represents anomalous threads with interactive timelines
allowing users to discover the trends and other temporal dynamics of information spreading
evidence.reasoning: The threads crop and the visual-encoding figure both describe a timeline-centered visual workspace with multiple thread representations.

subViews.description: Timeline canvas combining thread glyphs, thread timeline variants, time-axis controls, and optional user-link overlays for comparing multiple threads.

quotes: both leaf and internal nodes can be added to the threads view
multi-scale navigations along the time dimension
evidence.reasoning: The paper treats the thread glyphs, timelines, and overlays as one coordinated timeline region.

x.description: Horizontal position follows absolute time or aligned relative time across the thread timeline variants.

y.description: Vertical structure depends on the active thread representation, such as a volume summary or circle-packed user layout.

color.field: hidden state and user anomaly cues
color.description: Background state colors and user-level anomaly coloring appear within the supported timeline variants rather than one fixed simultaneous encoding stack.

source.description: Optional overlay links originate from one user in the estimated social connection graph.

target.description: Optional overlay links end at the connected user in the same or another thread.

quotes: three thread timeline visualizations for unfolding the temporal patterns with different perspectives
reveal the user social connections at the intra-or inter-thread level by overlaying links on the timeline views
evidence.reasoning: The paper supports a family of timeline representations plus an optional link overlay, but not one fixed simultaneous area-plus-points composition.

capabilities.description: Displays one or more retweeting threads as comparable timelines with hidden-state backgrounds and thread glyph summaries.

quotes: interactive timelines
both leaf and internal nodes can be added to the threads view
evidence.reasoning: The threads workspace primarily presents thread timelines for comparison.

capabilities.description: Lets the analyst brush and drag a time window to zoom and pan across thread timelines.

quotes: zooming and panning, can be easily operated by directly brushing and dragging a time window
evidence.reasoning: The threads view explicitly supports time-window navigation.

capabilities.description: Allows a loaded thread timeline to be split into multiple child threads in place.

quotes: dive into a thread timeline by splitting it into multiple child threads in-place
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly describes in-place expansion within the threads view.

capabilities.description: Highlights retweets corresponding to hovered descendant nodes from the overview views.

quotes: highlight retweets from each of its sub-threads
evidence.reasoning: The timelines visibly update to show retweets associated with overview interactions.

capabilities.description: Toggles highlights for users who appear multiple times within or across threads.

quotes: duplicated users within the same thread or across different threads can be highlighted by toggling a button
evidence.reasoning: This is an explicit user-triggered function of the threads view.

capabilities.description: Overlays social links between users within or across threads to expose possible relationships.

quotes: reveal the user social connections at the intra-or inter-thread level by overlaying links on the timeline views
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly describes a toggle that reveals user-link overlays in the timeline view.

capabilities.description: Reveals OCCRF hidden-state transitions as background color bands behind thread timelines.

quotes: hidden state transitions shown as the background of thread timeline views
evidence.reasoning: The threads view directly visualizes latent model states in the timeline background.

viewsInfo.description: Detail panel containing lower-level feature, state, and tweet views to inspect the model and selected threads more closely.

quotes: a detail information panel with three subviews containing d) a features view, e) a states view and f) a tweets view
evidence.reasoning: The interface caption explicitly names the panel and its three subviews.

subViews.description: Heatmap-like view summarizing temporal variation of feature vectors for selected threads.

quotes: the features view which summarizes the temporal variations of feature vectors ... with a heatmap-like visualization
evidence.reasoning: The features subview is explicitly named and described as a heatmap-like temporal summary.

x.description: Columns progress along time for the selected thread.

y.description: Rows correspond to individual feature dimensions.

color.description: Cell color encodes the temporal variation of each feature value.

quotes: heatmap-like visualization
heatmaps of feature values
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly characterizes the features view as a heatmap of temporal feature values.

capabilities.description: Shows how selected feature vectors vary over time for the current thread or comparison set.

quotes: summarizes the temporal variations of feature vectors
evidence.reasoning: The features view directly exposes temporal feature variation.

processingSteps.name: temporal feature-vector summarization
processingSteps.description: Aggregates thread feature vectors over time into a heatmap-like representation for inspecting temporal variation.
input: temporal feature vectors for selected threads

quotes: the features view which summarizes the temporal variations of feature vectors
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly ties temporal feature summarization to the features view.

subViews.description: Projection view of users associated with hidden states, with optional axis reassignment to specific features.

quotes: displaying the MDS projections of all users from the highdimensional feature space
evidence.reasoning: The states view is explicitly described as an MDS projection of users tied to hidden states.

x.description: The x-axis can show an MDS dimension or a user-selected feature.

y.description: The y-axis can show an MDS dimension or a user-selected feature.

color.description: Point groups correspond to hidden states characterizing user features.

quotes: forming a scatter-plot of users with the correlations between features
evidence.reasoning: The states view is a point-based projection/scatterplot used to inspect hidden-state signatures.

capabilities.description: Shows how users associated with hidden states are distributed in projected feature space.

quotes: the states view indicates how states are tied to tweet users
evidence.reasoning: The subview is designed to expose user-state relationships directly.

capabilities.description: Lets the analyst assign specific features to the axes, turning the view into a feature-correlation scatterplot.

quotes: the user can set the axes to represent specific features
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly describes axis reassignment in the states view.

processingSteps.name: multidimensional scaling (MDS)
processingSteps.description: Projects users from the high-dimensional feature space into a 2D layout so analysts can inspect how hidden states relate to user distributions.

quotes: displaying the MDS projections of all users from the highdimensional feature space
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly binds the user MDS projection to the states view.

subViews.description: Textual detail view for raw tweet contents associated with selected threads.

quotes: raw tweets view
From the raw tweets view, she sees the range of anomaly scores
evidence.reasoning: The panel's third named subview exposes raw tweets and thread values as textual details.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: List-like text panel for reading raw tweets and related thread values or metadata.

quotes: raw tweets view
evidence.reasoning: The tweets view is described as textual access to raw tweets rather than a chart.

capabilities.description: Lets the analyst inspect tweet contents and some associated values for the selected thread.

quotes: From the raw tweets view, she sees the range of anomaly scores
evidence.reasoning: The case-study passage confirms the tweets view is used for direct textual inspection.

viewsInfo.description: Auxiliary details-on-demand tooltip for thread metadata and keyword snippets.

quotes: informative tooltips
from the tooltip she can see a few interesting keywords
evidence.reasoning: The caption and case study both describe an auxiliary tooltip used to inspect thread metadata and keywords.

subViews.description: Small pop-up card showing thread metadata such as keywords, timestamps, or score values.

quotes: extra information of the thread nodes
from the tooltip she can see a few interesting keywords
evidence.reasoning: The tooltip appears as a compact detail card for a selected thread node.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Compact card-like textual detail surface for metadata and keyword snippets.

quotes: informative tooltips
evidence.reasoning: The tooltip is a transient textual inspector rather than a standalone chart.

capabilities.description: Displays keywords and other concise metadata for a hovered or inspected thread node.

quotes: access extra information of the thread nodes
see a few interesting keywords
evidence.reasoning: The tooltip’s direct role is to surface concise metadata.

viewsInfo.description: Auxiliary details-on-demand context menu for raw tweet contents, exact score values, and timestamps.

quotes: context menus
raw tweets shown in the context menu
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly labels a context menu as the surface for detailed tweet content and precise values.

subViews.description: Pop-up menu-like detail surface exposing raw tweets, exact score values, and starting or ending timestamps.

quotes: tweet contents, exact score values and starting/ending timestamps
evidence.reasoning: The context menu appears as a detailed pop-up tied to thread-node inspection.

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Inspector-like pop-up surface for reading thread-level textual details and exact values.

quotes: raw tweets shown in the context menu
evidence.reasoning: This auxiliary view behaves like a pop-up inspector rather than a persistent form or control.

capabilities.description: Displays raw tweet text together with exact score values and timestamps for an inspected thread node.

quotes: access extra information of the thread nodes
raw tweets shown in the context menu
evidence.reasoning: The context menu’s user-visible function is direct inspection of detailed thread contents and values.

quotes: nodes in collapsed branches in the cluster view are shown semi-transparently in the MDS view
evidence.reasoning: Collapsing or aggregating hierarchy structure in the cluster overview explicitly changes rendering of corresponding nodes in the MDS overview.

quotes: double-click a node in either to unfold the timeline visualization of that thread in the threads view

quotes: double-click a node in either to unfold the timeline visualization of that thread in the threads view

quotes: hovering over the descendant nodes in either the cluster view or the MDS view
evidence.reasoning: Hovering descendant nodes in the MDS view updates highlights in the thread timelines.

quotes: A coupled zooming mechanism with the threads view is also incorporated

quotes: Both views provide informative tooltips (Fig. 5-g) and context menus (Fig. 5-h) to access extra information of the thread nodes
evidence.reasoning: Inspecting a thread node in the cluster overview can open the tooltip for concise metadata details.
